document.docAuthor: Mérida-Rivas, Jorge A., Hinojosa-Díaz, Ismael A., Ayala-Barajas, Ricardo, Pozo, Carmen & Vandame, Rémy
document.masterDocTitle: Revision of carpenter bees of the subgenus Neoxylocopa Michener (Hymenoptera: Apidae) from Mexico and Mesoamerica

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Revision of carpenter bees of the subgenus Neoxylocopa Michener (Hymenoptera: Apidae) from Mexico and Mesoamerica

paragraph.treatmentCitationGroup:
Xylocopa fimbriata Fabricius, 1804: 340
. Holotype : University of Copenhagen Zoological Museum, Copenhagen, Denmark ( ♀ ). Type locality: ‘America meridionalis ’ [according to
Moure (1960: 144)
.

Xylocopa corniger Westwood, 1840: 270
. Holotype : Oxford University Museum Oxford. England ( ♀ ). Type locality: ‘un- known’, synonymy by
Smith (1874:284)
.

Xylocopa cajennae Lepeletier, 1841: 203
. Syntype : Oxford University Museum Oxford. England ( ♂ ). Type locality: French Guiana , Cayenne, synonymy by
Smith (1874:284)
.

Xylocopa cornuta Lepeletier, 1841: 176
. Syntype : Istituto di Zoologia dell’Universita di Torino, Torino, Italy ( ♀ ). Type locality: French Guiana , Cayenne, synonymy by
Erichson (1848:591)
.

Xylocopa virescens Lepeletier, 1841: 186
. Holotype : Istituto di Zoologia dell’Universita di Torino, Torino. Italy ( ♀ ). Type locality: French Guiana , Cayenne, synonymy by
Moure (1960:144)
.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Xylocopa fimbriata var. motaguensis
Cockerell, 1912
; 556. Holotype : American Museum of Natural History , New York , USA ( ♀ ). Type locality: Guatemala , Gualán.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURES 1–5. Female of
X. ( N. ) fimbriata
. 1. Frontal view; 2. Lateral habitus; 3. Dorsal habitus; 4. Basitibial plate; 5. Scutellum

subSubSection.paragraph:
Description . Female : Integument and pubescence black; wings brown to dark brown with blue-violet iridescence ( Fig. 3 ); venation dark brown. Body length 28.74 mm (27.62–30.60; n=5); length of forewing 25.27 mm (24.63–26.29; n=5); head length 7.18 mm (6.78–7.58; n=5); intertegular distance 8.37 mm (7.66–8.78; n=5); width of T2 12.48 mm (11.69–12.82; n=5). Head : Frontal area with two large prominent elevations similar to horns that arise between the lateral ocelli and middle ocellus; middle ocellus below upper level of eyes ( Fig. 1 ). Very wide inter-ocular space (wider than compound eye length). Clypeus with a large depression toward the middle of antertior submargin, with large dense punctures separated by less than their diameter, diminishing toward the inferolateral areas; inferior margin of the clypeus with punctures on its entire width, forming a margin with a width equal to the diameter of a puncture. Lateral ocelli located above tangent of eyes.
Mesosoma : Mesoscutal
disc unpunctate, area between parapsidal line and disc with some sparse punctation (punctures separated by three or more puncture diameters). Metasoma : T1 to T3 with scattered minute punctures. Length of hairs on terga respect MOD as follows: T2 to T4 with minute hairs, T5 0.49–1.02. Male : Pubescence and integument yellowish orange. Hyaline wings, venation reddish-brown. Body length 29.43 mm (28.45–30.97; n=5); length of forewing 23.75 mm (22.71–24.79; n=5); head length 4.98 mm (4.83–5.18; n=5); intertegular distance 8.47 mm (7.83–8.95; n=5); width of T2 13.74 mm (13.17–14.20; n=5). Head : Labrum with a triangular shaped area without punctures on the middle region; rest of labrum with scattered punctures. Clypeus with very small, scattered punctures on marginal, submarginal, and inferolateral regions; subapical region without punctures (some specimens with some very scattered punctures); clypeus disc and superior region with dense punctures, separated by three or more times their diameter. Lateral ocelli located on the same level as tangent of eyes.
Mesosoma : Between
the parapsidal line and discal area with dense punctures, separated by one to one and a half times their diameter. Posterior tibia: anterior margin with long light brown pubescence; posterior margin with long and short pubescence; internal surface with more abundant pubescence toward the basal area and short dense pubescence on the distal extreme; internal distal extreme with a notable sharp projection but with apex rounded; anterior margin with a rounded prominence; width of projection two thirds the width of the tibia ( Fig. 9 ). Metasoma : T1 with dense, long, plumose hairs, T2 to T5 with plumose hairs restricted to submarginal area, mixed with simple hairs, T6 and T7 with a mixture of dense, long, simple and plumose hairs. Length of hairs on terga respect MOD as follows: T2 0.71–1.59, T3 0.77–1.28, T4 0.79–1.42, T5 0.81–1.54. Genitalia : Distal angle of the ventral section of the gonostylus, acute ( Fig. 104 ); basal angle at the base of lateral process of the gonostylus, strong, very noticeable ( Fig. 103 ); tip of the lateral process ending broad, rhomboid shaped ( Fig. 103 ).

caption.paragraph:
FIGURES 6–10. Male of
X. ( N. ) fimbriata
. 6. Frontal view; 7. Lateral habitus; 8. Dorsal habitus; 9. Hind tibia; 10. Metasoma

subSubSection.paragraph: Variation . The elevations adjacent to the ocelli present variation in size among specimens collected in a single or different locality on the same date. Throughout its distribution, wings may have bluish iridiscence with violet or only bluish iridescence, with the papillate area with greenish iridescence. These variations have led to the fact that throughout history, different species have been described, which have been placed in synonymy. Metasoma: Some males have dark brown terga, perhaps due preservation issues in collections.

Commentary . In areas where species of the
frontalis
group are sympatric,
X. fimbriata
may be confused with
X. frontalis
and
X. nautlana
, from which it may be distinguished as it presents frontal elevational characteristics, the prominences adjacent to the ocelli in the form of horns; males are distinguished because they do not present black integument on any of the terga of the metasoma.
